question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: ImporterConfiguration (class-level @EnableAsync)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high volume of TOKENFREEZE transactions to the Hedera network, causing the async thread pool to be saturated with freeze processing tasks, starving TOKENUNFREEZE transaction handlers of threads and effectively causing permanent freezing of accounts due to processing starvation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp placeholder is injected as a raw Long.toString() value into Flyway SQL without sanitization, and if the value contains special characters due to a misconfigured property source, cause SQL injection in the migration that corrupts the token_account freeze_status column?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway HikariDataSource is created with setMaximumPoolSize(10) and setMinimumIdle(0), meaning all 10 connections can be acquired simultaneously, to perform a connection pool exhaustion attack that prevents the importer's main data source from processing TOKENUNFREEZE transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: ImporterConfiguration (class-level @EnableResilientMethods)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a malformed TOKENFREEZE transaction that causes a non-transient exception during processing, but @EnableResilientMethods retries it anyway due to misconfigured retry policy, causing the freeze to be applied multiple times and overwriting any intermediate TOKENUNFREEZE, permanently freezing the account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the flywayConfigurationCustomizer() bean is registered before FlywayAutoConfiguration runs (due to @AutoConfigureBefore), and if another FlywayConfigurationCustomizer bean is registered with higher priority, the topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp placeholder could be overwritten with an incorrect value, causing freeze_status migration corruption?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway DataSource is annotated with @Bean(defaultCandidate = false) and @FlywayDataSource, meaning it is only used by Flyway, to cause a scenario where the main application DataSource and the Flyway DataSource point to different databases, with Flyway running migrations on a stale database that has incorrect freeze_status values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that blockProperties.isEnabled() defaults to false and recordDownloaderProperties.isEnabled() defaults to true, and if a configuration injection causes blockProperties.isEnabled() to return true, the init() method throws IllegalStateException, halting the importer and preventing TOKENUNFREEZE transactions from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that dbWaiter.waitForDatabase() is called inside flywayDataSource() bean creation, and if the database becomes unavailable after the wait completes but before HikariDataSource is created, cause a partial initialization state where Flyway migrations are not run and token_account freeze_status remains in a FROZEN default state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: ImporterConfiguration (class-level @EnableAsync)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableAsync annotation to cause out-of-order processing of TOKENFREEZE and TOKENUNFREEZE transactions when they arrive in the same batch, where async execution reorders them so TOKENFREEZE is applied after TOKENUNFREEZE, permanently freezing the account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the timestamp comparison in flywayConfigurationCustomizer() uses importerProperties.getNetwork() which calls StringUtils.substringBefore(this.network, '-').toLowerCase(), and if the network property is set to an empty string, the comparison fails and the non-mainnet timestamp 1588706343553042000L is used, causing incorrect freeze_status migration on mainnet?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway HikariDataSource uses owner-level credentials (dbProperties.getOwner() / dbProperties.getOwnerPassword()) which have DDL privileges, and if these credentials are exposed through a misconfigured endpoint, execute arbitrary SQL that sets all token_account freeze_status values to FROZEN?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: SchedulingConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the comment 'This toggle exists only to disable scheduling for test execution and shouldn't be modified by operators' to identify that spring.task.scheduling.enabled=false is a known configuration path, and if they can influence environment variables (e.g., SPRING_TASK_SCHEDULING_ENABLED=false), disable scheduling and prevent TOKENUNFREEZE transactions from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp is a nanosecond-precision timestamp (1592499600000000000L = ~June 2020), and by submitting transactions with consensus timestamps in this range, cause the Flyway migration to misclassify their running_hash_version, leading to incorrect freeze_status processing for those transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that hikariConfig.copyStateTo(flywayHikariConfig) copies all HikariCP settings including connection timeout, and if the base hikariConfig has a very short connectionTimeout, cause the Flyway DataSource to fail to acquire connections during freeze_status migration, leaving the migration incomplete and accounts in a FROZEN state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: ImporterConfiguration (class-level @EnableResilientMethods)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableResilientMethods retry behavior to cause a TOKENUNFREEZE transaction handler to fail on retry after a transient error, while a subsequent TOKENFREEZE transaction succeeds, resulting in the account being permanently FROZEN because the unfreeze was dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the flywayConfigurationCustomizer() lambda captures importerProperties by reference, and if importerProperties.getNetwork() or importerProperties.getTopicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp() changes between application startup and Flyway migration execution, cause the wrong timestamp to be used, corrupting freeze_status data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway DataSource pool name is set to hikariConfig.getPoolName() + '_flyway', and if the base pool name is excessively long, cause a pool name truncation or exception that prevents the Flyway DataSource from initializing, halting migrations and leaving token_account freeze_status in a FROZEN default state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that init() is called after all beans are constructed but before the application is fully started, and if a race condition causes blockProperties.isEnabled() to return true transiently during startup, trigger the IllegalStateException that prevents the importer from processing any TOKENUNFREEZE transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: ImporterConfiguration (class-level @EnableAsync)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableAsync annotation to cause a thread pool exhaustion scenario where all async threads are blocked processing TOKENFREEZE transactions, preventing TOKENUNFREEZE transactions from being scheduled, effectively causing permanent freezing of accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway placeholder topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp is used in the SQL condition 'consensus_timestamp < ${topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp}', and if the timestamp is set to 0 (due to a null network property), cause all topic_message rows to be assigned running_hash_version=2, corrupting the data pipeline that feeds into freeze_status processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway DataSource is created with new HikariDataSource(flywayHikariConfig) which validates the configuration at construction time, and if the jdbcUrl contains malformed parameters, cause a validation exception that prevents Flyway from running freeze_status migrations, leaving accounts in a FROZEN state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: ImporterConfiguration (class-level @EnableResilientMethods)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableResilientMethods annotation combined with @EnableAsync to create a scenario where a retried TOKENFREEZE transaction is processed asynchronously after the original TOKENUNFREEZE has been committed, causing the account to be re-frozen permanently?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the network name is compared using equalsIgnoreCase but the network value is processed through StringUtils.substringBefore(this.network, '-').toLowerCase(), meaning a network value of 'mainnet-' (with trailing delimiter) would return an empty string that does not match MAINNET, causing the wrong timestamp to be used and corrupting freeze_status migration?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway DataSource uses setIdleTimeout(60000) (60 seconds) which is shorter than typical long-running Flyway migrations, causing connections to be evicted mid-migration during the token_account freeze_status update, leaving the migration in a partially applied state where some accounts are FROZEN?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the IllegalStateException thrown by init() propagates up through the Spring context initialization, causing the entire application to fail to start, and since the importer is the only component that processes TOKENUNFREEZE transactions, all previously frozen accounts remain permanently frozen?
